source.data_access_pattern: bcftools remote query of a bounded chr22 interval; the selected sample list, subset VCF, index cache, and per-sample vectors are kept under ignored work/
source.data_reuse_statement: IGSR provides open data to support research; users should consult IGSR terms/data reuse statements and cite associated publications.

sources_rejected.reason: often controlled-access and unnecessary for this public AF panel
excellent benchmark truth data, but not a multi-population cohort suited to cross-population AF/FST-ish summaries

source_superpop_af_range_filter.definition: max(INFO.AFR_AF, AMR_AF, EAS_AF, EUR_AF, SAS_AF) - min(INFO.AFR_AF, AMR_AF, EAS_AF, EUR_AF, SAS_AF)

source.sample_selection_rule: for each super-population in order AFR, AMR, EAS, EUR, SAS, take a deterministic round-robin over alphabetically sorted 1000 Genomes population codes, preserving panel order within each population, until 10 samples are selected
source.variant_selection_rule: within the chr22 interval, keep the first 24 complete-call biallelic SNPs with IGSR global AF between 0.05 and 0.95, source super-population AF range >= 0.15, nonzero/nonfixed alternate-allele count in the selected cohort, and at least 200 bp from the prior kept variant

ethics_boundary.data_type: publicly available human genotype VCF subset under IGSR/1000G terms
ethics_boundary.committed_outputs: aggregate-only allele-frequency, group-frequency, FST-ish, application-output, provenance, and verification summaries
ethics_boundary.ignored_outputs: selected sample IDs, per-sample genotype vectors, subset VCF, remote index cache, and runtime diagnostics under work/
ethics_boundary.small_cell_policy: group-level allele counts, frequencies, source-reference AFs, and deltas are suppressed when n < 10
limitations: small deterministic workflow panel, not a population estimate
FST-ish values are equal-weight heterozygosity contrasts, not formal inference
public genomic data is not anonymous and still carries re-identification risk
